records.ety: [F. duplicit, L. duplicitas, fr. duplex double. See Duplex.]

quotes: Do not affect duplicities nor triplicities, nor any certain number of parts in your division of things.

defs: Doubleness of heart or speech; insincerity; a sustained form of deception which consists in entertaining or pretending to entertain one set of feelings, and acting as if influenced by another; bad faith.
quotes: Far from the duplicity wickedly charged on him, he acted his part with alacrity and resolution.

defs: The use of two or more distinct allegations or answers, where one is sufficient.
In indictments, the union of two incompatible offenses.
